page: OVHcloud a10-90 is a GPU-accelerated server within the Cloud GPU family, utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 60 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9554 processor running at a base frequency of 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system features 85.0 GB of system memory, yielding a allocation of 1.42 GB per core, alongside 400 GB of local SSD storage. Graphics and parallel processing acceleration are delivered by two NVIDIA A10 GPUs from the Ampere family, providing a combined total of 48 GB of VRAM. Network connectivity is supported by a 16 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple compute categories. Multi-core CPU capabilities are highlighted by top-decile results in stress-ng multi-core testing and Passmark CPU scores, alongside strong single-core performance. The dual NVIDIA A10 GPUs enable top-tier performance in large language model inference tasks, including prompt processing and text generation across 135M, 7B, and 70B parameter models. Memory performance is mixed, showing top-tier bandwidth for small-block cached reads but dropping to average levels for large-block uncached reads.

From a resource density perspective, the server offers high compute and GPU capacity relative to its hardware profile, though the memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1.42 GB per core represents a distinct tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. This hardware configuration is highly suited for GPU-accelerated workloads such as machine learning inference, ray tracing, and multi-threaded data decompression. It is also well-suited for parallel computing tasks, software compilation, and high-throughput database operations.
description: The OVHcloud a10-90 is a dedicated GPU-accelerated server designed for parallel processing and compute-intensive workloads. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9554 processor, it features 60 dedicated physical cores running at 3.3 GHz, paired with 85.0 GB of system memory and 400 GB of local SSD storage. Acceleration is driven by two NVIDIA A10 Ampere GPUs providing a total of 48 GB of VRAM. In benchmark testing, the server excels in multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and LLM inference across small, medium, and large models. While it delivers top-tier compute and graphics capabilities, its memory-to-core ratio of 1.42 GB represents a tradeoff for memory-bound tasks. This configuration is highly suited for machine learning inference, ray tracing, and parallelized data processing.
og_description: OVHcloud a10-90 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 60 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 85 GB of RAM, 400 GB of local SSD storage, and two NVIDIA A10 GPUs with 48 GB of total VRAM for parallel computing workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud a10-90 is a GPU-accelerated server with 60 AMD EPYC cores, 85 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA A10 GPUs designed for machine learning and compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute platform featuring dual NVIDIA A10 GPUs and sixty dedicated AMD EPYC cores for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: Sixty dedicated AMD EPYC 9554 physical cores running at 3.3 GHz
Dual NVIDIA A10 Ampere GPUs with a combined 48 GB of VRAM
Top-tier performance in LLM inference and multi-core CPU benchmarks
400 GB of local SSD storage and 16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Suited for machine learning inference, ray tracing, and software compilation
